author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
He Meets Pelagius with Another Passage from Hilary.
title: He Meets Pelagius with Another Passage from Hilary.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Now even Job himself is not silent respecting his own sins; and your friend, of course, is justly of opinion that humility must not by any means “be put on the side of falsehood.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Whatever confession, therefore, Job makes, inasmuch as he is a true worshipper of God, he undoubtedly makes it in truth.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Hilary, likewise, while expounding that passage of the psalm in which it is written, “Thou hast despised all those who turn aside from Thy commandments,” says: “If God were to despise sinners, He would despise indeed all men, because no man is without sin; but it is those who turn away from Him, whom they call apostates, that He despises.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: You observe his statement: it is not to the effect that no man was without sin, as if he spoke of the past; but no man is without sin; and on this point, as I have already remarked, I have no contention with him.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: But if one refuses to submit to the Apostle John,—who does not himself declare, “If we were to say we have had no sin,” but “If we say we have no sin,”—how is he likely to show deference to Bishop Hilary?

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: It is in defence of the grace of Christ that I lift up my voice, without which grace no man is justified,—just as if natural free will were sufficient.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Nay, He Himself lifts up His own voice in defence of the same.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Let us submit to Him when He says: “Without me ye can do nothing.”
